question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a partition-like state freeze for `TokenService` by triggering `getReadableStates(TokenService.NAME)` to cache a `MapReadableStates` that wraps `STAKING_NETWORK_REWARDS_STATE_ID` as a `FunctionReadableSingletonState` (line 82), and then exploiting the fact that the underlying `DefaultSingleton` always returns a default value, so post-partition staking reward queries always return stale/default data?
